content:
Yes, I’m sure. Cepheid variable stars are a key "standard candle" in astronomy because of the well-established relationship between their pulsation period and intrinsic luminosity (the period-luminosity relation). By measuring the period of a Cepheid's brightness variations, astronomers can determine its absolute magnitude, compare it to its apparent magnitude, and calculate the distance to that star—and by extension, to the galaxy in which it resides.

This method is fundamental for measuring distances beyond our own galaxy, making option (B) the correct choice.
